title: Deep Fried Japanese Chicken Gyoza
description: A malty, cheese-pull Japanese dish featuring Chinese sesame sauce, perfect for a hangover cure, made with whole30-friendly ingredients. This agemono-deep-fry recipe is a twist on traditional gyoza.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground chicken, sesame oil, Chinese sesame sauce, scallions, garlic, and ginger, mixing well.
Add coconut flour and mix until just combined.
Add eggs and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Heat about 2-3 inches of sesame oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Carefully add the gyoza to the hot oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Remove the gyoza from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Drizzle with malt syrup and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
